name: rad-okf
description:
Build and maintain an Open Knowledge Format (OKF) knowledge base from Claude Code — for developers consolidating a repo's knowledge and for non-technical knowledge workers building an AI-readable second brain. OKF bundles are plain directories of markdown files with YAML frontmatter: human-readable, git-friendly, and consumable by any AI agent without an SDK or server.

The plugin keeps a bundle healthy and hard to let rot, with a dependency-free stdlib Python 3 engine that never overwrites your files without a preview.

v0.1 (available now): `check` validates conformance (frontmatter + required `type`, broken cross-links, orphaned and stale concepts); `map` generates a self-contained, zero-dependency HTML graph. Landing in subsequent releases: authoring (`start` guided onboarding, `new`, `add`, link-safe `move`) and ingestion (`convert` non-markdown, `scan` a repo or folder for importable knowledge, `find` search), plus an always-on `okf` conventions skill. Sync-agnostic — manage the knowledge here, sync the folder however you like (git, cloud folder, Obsidian, Syncthing).
